title: lichen_l2.c:1003 send calls net_pkt_unref but ownership transfer unclear
description: Contract documentation: lichen_l2_send() - Comments at lines 996-1002 explain ownership transfer on success. But if send() is interrupted (thread abort) between line 980 (lichen_lora_l2_tx success) and line 1003 (net_pkt_unref), the packet leaks. This is unlikely (no yield point between TX and unref) but theoretically possible with k_thread_abort(). WHY: Memory leak on abort. HOW to fix: Document this as a known limitation of thread abort, or use atomic packet ownership tracking. Very low priority.
